heading: Deferral of duty on large yachts imported for sale at United States boat shows
label: 1484b Deferral of duty on large yachts imported for sale at United States boat shows

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, any vessel meeting the definition of a large yacht as provided in subsection (b) and which is otherwise dutiable may be imported without the payment of duty if imported with the intention to offer for sale at a boat show in the United States. Payment of duty shall be deferred, in accordance with this section, until such large yacht is sold.

children.heading: Definition
children.label: b Definition
children.text: As used in this section, the term “large yacht” means a vessel that exceeds 79 feet in length, is used primarily for recreation or pleasure, and has been previously sold by a manufacturer or dealer to a retail consumer.
certifies to the Customs Service that the large yacht is imported pursuant to this section for sale at a boat show in the United States; and

posts a bond, which shall have a duration of 6 months after the date of importation, in an amount equal to twice the amount of duty on the large yacht that would otherwise be imposed under subheading 8903.91.00 or 8903.92.00 of the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

children.heading: Deposit of duty1So in original. No par. (2) has been enacted.1
children.label: 1 Deposit of duty1So in original. No par. (2) has been enacted.1
A
children.text: entry shall be completed and duty (calculated at the applicable rates provided for under subheading 8903.91.00 or 8903.92.00 of the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States and based upon the value of the large yacht at the time of importation) shall be deposited with the Customs Service; and

children.label: B
children.text: the bond posted as required by subsection (c)(2) shall be returned to the importer.

children.heading: Procedures upon expiration of bond period
children.label: e Procedures upon expiration of bond period
A
children.text: entry shall be completed and duty (calculated at the applicable rates provided for under subheading 8903.91.00 or 8903.92.00 of the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States and based upon the value of the large yacht at the time of importation) shall be deposited with the Customs Service; and

children.label: B
children.text: the bond posted as required by subsection (c)(2) shall be returned to the importer.

children.heading: Additional requirements
children.label: 2 Additional requirements
children.text: No extensions of the bond period shall be allowed. Any large yacht exported in compliance with the bond period may not be reentered for purposes of sale at a boat show in the United States (in order to receive duty deferral benefits) for a period of 3 months after such exportation.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: f Regulations
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury is authorized to make such rules and regulations as may be necessary to carry out the provisions of this section.
